purpose: Define Phase A liquidation and payment readiness for shopper honorarium, reimbursement, June cutoff, historical pending payments and multi-project operation without executing real payments.
eligibilityRules: Visit performed alone does not make liquidation eligible.
Questionnaire completed alone does not make liquidation eligible.
Submitido/review policy must be satisfied before candidate_ready unless explicit admin exception exists.
June visits already executed should be handled as payment/liquidation pending, not as visits pending execution.
Historical pending payments must remain importable into payment batches without duplicating new visits.
Multi-project and multi-country amounts must use project/country/currency configuration or HR mapped values.
Honorarium and reimbursement are separate amounts. Reimbursement may be zero/not applicable.
batchRules: A payment batch can include items from same cutoff and may allow later additions if not closed.
Items from previous months can be included if pending.
Future-month items must remain separate unless admin intentionally adds them according to policy.
Batch must preserve createdBy, approvedBy, paidBy future/manual, dates and auditTrail.
No real money movement is executed by this contract.
shopperVisibleRules: Shopper may see honorarium, reimbursement, expected/payment status and relevant history.
Shopper must not see internal audit notes, bank data or sensitive admin comments.
Mis beneficios should separate earned, pending, scheduled and paid amounts.

newLessons: Difference between performed, questionnaire completed, submitido, liquidation and payment
How June executed visits become payment pending
What shoppers can see in Mis beneficios

hardStops: Do not execute real payments from this contract.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
Do not write HR from this contract.
Do not expose bank data in shopper-visible views.
Do not mark paid without explicit future/manual payment confirmation.
Do not treat questionnaire completed as payable without submitido/review policy.
